title: Working with link functions
text: link created constructor prints name, domain, parameters: two directions derivatives generics dispatch link: forward derivatives taken respect θ\theta, inverse ones respect η\eta: exact, finite differences. log link g(θ)=log⁡θg(\theta) = \log\theta g′(θ)=1/θg'(\theta) = 1/\theta, indeed:
code: log_link() #> S7 Link Object: log #>   - Parameter domain (theta): (0, Inf) logit_link() #> S7 Link Object: logit #>   - Parameter domain (theta): (0, 1) lk <- log_link() theta <- c(0.5, 1, 2)  linkfun(lk, theta)       # eta = g(theta) = log(theta) #> [1] -0.6931472  0.0000000  0.6931472 linkinv(lk, linkfun(lk, theta))   # back to theta #> [1] 0.5 1.0 2.0 eta <- linkfun(lk, theta)  dlinkfun(lk, theta)      # g'(theta) #> [1] 2.0 1.0 0.5 dlinkinv(lk, eta)        # (g^{-1})'(eta) #> [1] 0.5 1.0 2.0 all.equal(dlinkfun(lk, theta), 1 / theta) #> [1] TRUE

title: Working with link functions
text: new link subclass link ten methods: two directions four derivatives . pattern short; complete one negative log-log link, η=−log⁡(−log⁡θ)\eta = -\log(-\log\theta) (0,1)(0, 1). derivatives come right? Ask diagnostic rather trusting algebra: line came back [FAILED], derivative revisit — exactly mistake check_link() exists catch.
code: NegLogLog <- S7::new_class("NegLogLog", parent = link)  S7::method(linkfun, NegLogLog)  <- function(x, theta) -log(-log(theta)) S7::method(linkinv, NegLogLog)  <- function(x, eta)   exp(-exp(-eta))  S7::method(dlinkfun,  NegLogLog) <- function(x, theta) -1 / (theta * log(theta)) S7::method(d2linkfun, NegLogLog) <- function(x, theta) (1 + log(theta)) / (theta * log(theta))^2 S7::method(d3linkfun, NegLogLog) <- function(x, theta) {   l <- log(theta)   -(2 * l^2 + 3 * l + 2) / (theta * l)^3 } S7::method(d4linkfun, NegLogLog) <- function(x, theta) {   l <- log(theta)   (6 * l^3 + 11 * l^2 + 12 * l + 6) / (theta * l)^4 }  S7::method(dlinkinv,  NegLogLog) <- function(x, eta) exp(-eta - exp(-eta)) S7::method(d2linkinv, NegLogLog) <- function(x, eta) exp(-eta - exp(-eta)) * (exp(-eta) - 1) S7::method(d3linkinv, NegLogLog) <- function(x, eta) {   e <- exp(-eta)   exp(-eta - e) * (e^2 - 3 * e + 1) } S7::method(d4linkinv, NegLogLog) <- function(x, eta) {   e <- exp(-eta)   exp(-eta - e) * (e^3 - 6 * e^2 + 7 * e - 1) }  neglog <- NegLogLog(link_name = "neglog-log", link_bounds = c(0, 1), link_params = NULL) invisible(check_link(neglog)) #> Checking S7 Link Object: neglog-log  #>   [1] Invertibility (Theta space): [PASSED]  #>   [2] Invertibility (Eta space):   [PASSED]  #>   [3] Strict Monotonicity:         [PASSED]  #>   [4] Inverse Function Theorem:    [PASSED]  #>   [5] Link Derivatives:            [PASSED]  #>   [6] Inverse Link Derivatives:    [PASSED]

title: The General Bounded Link Function — bounded_link
The General Bounded Link Function — bounded_link
text: lwr Numeric NULL. lower bound interval. upr Numeric NULL. upper bound interval.

title: The General Bounded Link Function — bounded_link
text: S7 object class link containing transformation functions exact analytical derivatives fourth order.

title: The General Bounded Link Function — bounded_link
text: Doubly Bounded (lwr upr provided): Transforms \(\theta\) normalizing c(0, 1) via \(p = \frac{\theta - \text{lwr}}{\text{upr} - \text{lwr}}\), applying logit function. Lower Bounded (lwr provided, upr = NULL): Defined \(\eta = \log(\theta - \text{lwr})\), inverse \(\theta = \exp(\eta) + \text{lwr}\). Upper Bounded (lwr = NULL, upr provided): Defined \(\eta = \log(\text{upr} - \theta)\), inverse \(\theta = \text{upr} - \exp(\eta)\). Unbounded (lwr = NULL, upr = NULL): Returns standard identity_link.

title: The Cauchit Link Function — cauchit_link
text: Cauchit link defined mathematically \(\eta = \tan(\pi(\theta - 0.5))\), corresponds perfectly qcauchy(theta). inverse link standard Cauchy CDF \(\theta = \frac{1}{\pi} \arctan(\eta) + 0.5\), computed via pcauchy(eta). Heavy Tails: Unlike Logit Probit links, Cauchit link exceedingly heavier tails. makes particularly robust useful modeling binary data probability approaches 0 1 slowly, dataset contains severe outliers might disproportionately influence fit light-tailed link functions. strictly valid mathematical domain \(\theta\) c(0, 1).

what: Validate and Check a Link Object — check_link
title: Validate and Check a Link Object — check_link
text: diagnostic S7 method mathematically validate link object. sequentially verifies algebraic invertibility correctness analytical derivatives using numerical gradients chained sequence.

title: Validate and Check a Link Object — check_link
Validate and Check a Link Object — check_link
text: x object class link. tolerance Numeric tolerance floating-point comparisons. ... Additional arguments passed methods.

title: Validate and Check a Link Object — check_link
text: logical list returning success status available checks.

title: Validate and Check a Link Object — check_link
text: function assumes existence S7 generics linkfun, linkinv, linkderiv, linkinvderiv. method performs following six diagnostic checks: Invertibility (\(\theta\) space): Verifies \(g^{-1}(g(\theta)) = \theta\). Ensures mapping parameter space linear predictor back lossless. Invertibility (\(\eta\) space): Verifies \(g(g^{-1}(\eta)) = \eta\). Ensures mapping linear predictor parameter space back lossless. Note test may fail intentionally correctly links map restricted \(\eta\) domain (e.g., square root link). Strict Monotonicity: Checks first derivative \(g'(\theta)\) strictly positive strictly negative across domain, guaranteeing one--one mapping. Inverse Function Theorem: Verifies mathematical identity \(g'(\theta) \cdot (g^{-1})'(\eta) = 1\), confirming theoretical relationship link derivative inverse link derivative. Link Derivatives: Validates exact analytical forward derivatives \(g(\theta)\) 4th order comparing numerical gradients. Inverse Link Derivatives: Validates exact analytical inverse derivatives \(g^{-1}(\eta)\) 4th order comparing numerical gradients. forward inverse derivative testing avoids compounding numerical errors applying first-order numerical differentiation iteratively exact lower-order analytical derivatives.

title: The Complementary Log-Log (ClogLog) Link Function — cloglog_link
text: ClogLog link defined mathematically \(\eta = \log(-\log(1 - \theta))\). Consequently, inverse link derived \(\theta = 1 - \exp(-\exp(\eta))\). Unlike symmetric Logit Probit links, ClogLog link lacks symmetry. fundamentally related Extreme Value (Gumbel) distribution frequently utilized discrete-time survival analysis (proportional hazards models) well modeling rare events. strictly valid mathematical domain \(\theta\) c(0, 1).

title: The Identity Link Function — identity_link
text: Identity link defined simply \(\eta = \theta\). Consequently, inverse link also \(\theta = \eta\). first derivatives constant (equal 1), higher-order derivatives fourth order exactly zero. domain \(\theta\) unbounded, meaning valid domain c(-Inf, Inf).

title: The Inverse (Reciprocal) Link Function — inverse_link
text: Inverse link defined \(\eta = 1/\theta\). inverse link function therefore perfectly symmetric: \(\theta = 1/\eta\). link typically used modeling positive continuous data mean inversely proportional linear predictor (e.g., Gamma regression). domain \(\theta\) conventionally c(0, Inf). Care must taken ensure linear predictor \(\eta\) remains strictly positive (strictly negative) optimization avoid division zero mapping invalid negative parameter values.

title: The Inverse Square Link Function — inverse_sq_link
text: Inverse Square link defined mathematically \(\eta = 1 / \theta^2\). Consequently, inverse link function derived \(\theta = 1 / \sqrt{\eta}\). specific link function predominantly utilized Generalized Linear Models (GLMs) assuming Inverse Gaussian response distribution. frameworks, variance proportional cube mean (\(\text{Var}(Y) \propto \mu^3\)). Domain Optimization Constraints: parameter \(\theta\) linear predictor \(\eta\) must strictly positive. valid mathematical domain \(\theta\) c(0, Inf). optimization routines (e.g., Fisher Scoring Newton-Raphson), extreme care must taken ensure linear predictor \(\eta > 0\). Evaluating inverse link derivatives non-positive values \(\eta\) inevitably result NaNs due fractional powers square root operations.

title: S7 Class for Statistical Link Functions — link

title: The Logarithmic Link Function — log_link
text: Log link defined mathematically \(\eta = \log(\theta)\). inverse link exponential function \(\theta = \exp(\eta)\). remarkable mathematical property link inverse function derivative. Therefore, parameter \(\theta\) derivatives respect \(\eta\) equal \(\exp(\eta)\). valid mathematical domain \(\theta\) c(0, Inf). Numerical Stability: evaluation inverse link derivatives, result bounded .Machine$double.eps. prevents numerical underflow exactly zero \(\eta\) large negative number, otherwise produce Inf subsequently calculating \(1/\theta\).

title: The Logit Link Function — logit_link

title: The Logit Link Function — logit_link

title: The Logit Link Function — logit_link
text: Logit link defined mathematically \(\eta = \log(\frac{\theta}{1 - \theta})\). inverse link standard logistic function (sigmoid): \(\theta = \frac{1}{1 + \exp(-\eta)}\). link perfectly symmetric around \(\theta = 0.5\) (corresponds \(\eta = 0\)). elegantly interprets linear predictor log-odds event probability. mathematical domain \(\theta\) c(0, 1). Implementation Details: function internally delegates R's highly optimized native functions stats::qlogis stats::plogis. ensures maximum numerical stability precision, especially evaluating probabilities exceedingly close boundaries 0 1.

title: The Log-Log Link Function — loglog_link
text: Creates S7 object class link implementing Log-Log transformation. link asymmetric opposite cloglog link often used survival analysis (e.g., Gompertz models) modeling binary data specific asymmetry requirements.

title: The Log-Log Link Function — loglog_link
text: S7 object class LogLogLink (inheriting link) containing transformation functions exact analytical derivatives fourth order.

title: The Log-Log Link Function — loglog_link
text: Log-Log link mathematically defined \(\eta = -\log(-\log(\theta))\). Consequently, inverse link derived \(\theta = \exp(-\exp(-\eta))\). Asymmetry: Unlike symmetric Logit Probit links, Log-Log link highly asymmetric. particularly suitable modeling events probability approaches 0 slowly approaches 1 sharply. represents mathematical reverse cloglog link. strict mathematical domain \(\theta\) c(0, 1).

title: The Power Link Function — power_link
text: Creates S7 object class link implementing Power transformation family. function generates specific link function based provided power parameter lambda. elegantly includes special case lambda = 0, dynamically returns Log link.

title: The Power Link Function — power_link

title: The Power Link Function — power_link
text: S7 object class PowerLink (inheriting link), object class LogLink lambda = 0.

title: The Power Link Function — power_link
text: Power link defined mathematically \(\eta = \theta^\lambda\). Consequently, inverse link derived \(\theta = \eta^{1/\lambda}\). Special Case (Box-Cox continuity): lambda = 0, function mathematically approaches \(\log(\theta)\). scenario, function automatically instantiates returns log_link object, modifying internal state reflect lambda = 0 parameter. Common special cases include: lambda = 1: Identity link. lambda = 0.5: Square-root link. lambda = -1: Inverse link. lambda = 0: Log link. mathematical domain \(\theta\) c(0, Inf). Depending value lambda, extreme care must taken numerical optimization guarantee \(\eta\) remains strictly positive avoid NaNs fractional exponents.

title: The Probit Link Function — probit_link
text: Creates S7 object class link implementing Probit transformation. link function rigorously relies cumulative distribution function (CDF) standard Normal distribution. widely used Generalized Linear Models (GLMs) binary data, often justified latent normal variable interpretation.

title: The Probit Link Function — probit_link
text: S7 object class ProbitLink (inheriting link) containing transformation functions exact analytical derivatives fourth order.

title: The Probit Link Function — probit_link
text: Probit link mathematically defined \(\eta = \Phi^{-1}(\theta)\), \(\Phi^{-1}\) quantile function standard normal distribution (qnorm). inverse link \(\theta = \Phi(\eta)\), standard normal CDF (pnorm). Similarly logit link, Probit symmetric around \(\theta = 0.5\) (\(\eta = 0\)). However, tails Normal distribution approach 0 1 faster Logistic distribution. strictly mathematical domain \(\theta\) c(0, 1).

title: The Rhobit (Fisher's z) Link Function — rhobit_link

title: The Rhobit (Fisher's z) Link Function — rhobit_link
The Rhobit (Fisher's z) Link Function — rhobit_link
text: S7 object class RhobitLink (inheriting link) containing transformation functions exact analytical derivatives fourth order.

title: The Rhobit (Fisher's z) Link Function — rhobit_link
text: Rhobit link defined mathematically using inverse hyperbolic tangent function: \(\eta = \text{arctanh}(\theta) = \frac{1}{2} \log\left(\frac{1 + \theta}{1 - \theta}\right)\). inverse link hyperbolic tangent function: \(\theta = \tanh(\eta) = \frac{\exp(2\eta) - 1}{\exp(2\eta) + 1}\). valid mathematical domain \(\theta\) exactly c(-1, 1).

title: The Softplus Link Function — softplus_link
text: Creates S7 object class link implementing Softplus transformation. Softplus function smooth approximation rectifier (ReLU) ensures parameter \(\theta\) remains positive. Unlike Log link, implies exponential relationship throughout, Softplus link asymptotically approaches linearity large positive values linear predictor.

title: The Softplus Link Function — softplus_link
text: numeric value specifying scaling parameter (smoothness/steepness). Must strictly positive. Defaults 1.

title: The Softplus Link Function — softplus_link
text: S7 object class SoftplusLink (inheriting link) containing transformation functions, exact analytical derivatives fourth order, parameter .

title: The Softplus Link Function — softplus_link
text: Softplus link describes relationship response parameter \(\theta\) Softplus linear predictor \(\eta\). Mathematically: Inverse Link (Softplus): \(\theta = \frac{1}{} \log(1 + \exp(\eta))\) Link Function: \(\eta = \frac{1}{} \log(\exp(\theta) - 1)\) Behavior: large negative \(\eta\), \(\theta \approx 0\). large positive \(\eta\), \(\theta \approx \eta\) (linear behavior), whereas Log link imply \(\theta = \exp(\eta)\) (exponential behavior). Numerical Stability: inverse link implementation intelligently utilizes conditional algebraic logic ensure robust numerical stability large positive values \(\eta\), entirely avoiding precision overflow. mathematical domain \(\theta\) c(0, Inf).

The Square Root Link Function — sqrt_link

title: The Square Root Link Function — sqrt_link
text: Square Root link mathematically defined \(\eta = \sqrt{\theta}\). Consequently, inverse link derived \(\theta = \eta^2\). Unlike Log link, transformation allows \(\theta\) reach 0 exactly. inverse function (\(\eta^2\)) mathematically valid negative values \(\eta\), specific context link function, linear predictor \(\eta\) typically constrained non-negative. restriction preserves strictly one--one mapping \(\theta\). strict mathematical domain \(\theta\) c(0, Inf).
